bibliographic_data.title: Spatial Mixing for Independent Sets in Poisson Random Trees
bibliographic_data.abstract: We consider correlation decay in the hard-core model with fugacity λ on a rooted tree T in which the arity of each vertex is independently Poisson distributed with mean d. Specifically, we investigate the question of which parameter settings (d, λ) result in strong spatial mixing, weak spatial mixing, or neither. For finite fugacity, a zero-one law implies that these spatial mixing properties hold either almost surely or almost never, once we have conditioned on whether T is finite or infinite. We provide a partial answer to this question, which implies in particular that 1) as d →∞, weak spatial mixing on the Poisson tree occurs whenever λ < f(d) −o(1) but not when λ is slightly above f(d), where f(d) is the threshold for WSM (and SSM) on the d-regular tree. This suggests that, in most cases, Poisson trees have similar spatial mixing behavior to regular trees; 2) when 1 < d ≤1.179, there is weak spatial mixing on the Poisson(d) tree for all values of λ. However, strong spatial mixing does not hold for sufficiently large λ. This is in contrast to regular trees, for which strong spatial mixing and weak spatial mixing always coincide. For infinite fugacity SSM holds only when the tree is finite, and hence almost surely fails on the Poisson(d) tree when d > 1. We show that WSM almost surely holds on the Poisson(d) tree for d < e^(1/√2), but it fails with positive probability if d > e.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper provides a partial answer to the question of which parameter settings (d, λ) result in strong spatial mixing or weak spatial mixing for independent sets in Poisson random trees.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors investigate the hard-core model with fugacity λ on a rooted tree T where each vertex has an independently Poisson distributed number of children. They use zero-one laws and analyze the behavior as d approaches infinity to derive conditions for weak spatial mixing (WSM) and strong spatial mixing (SSM).
outcomes: As d →∞, WSM on the Poisson tree occurs whenever λ < f(d) −o(1) but not when λ is slightly above f(d), where f(d) is the threshold for WSM (and SSM) on the d-regular tree.
When 1 < d ≤1.179, there is weak spatial mixing on the Poisson(d) tree for all values of λ, but strong spatial mixing does not hold for sufficiently large λ.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper investigates correlation decay in the hard-core model with fugacity λ on a rooted tree T where each vertex has an independently Poisson distributed number of children. The authors provide conditions under which weak spatial mixing (WSM) or strong spatial mixing (SSM) occurs for different parameter settings (d, λ). Specifically, they show that as d approaches infinity, WSM holds when λ is below a certain threshold f(d), but not above it. For smaller values of d, the Poisson tree exhibits WSM for all values of λ up to a point where strong mixing fails.
